blockingIssues.description: The timestamp fix adds a required `created_at` column to `skill_installations` but never migrates existing databases. Because `init_database()` only runs `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`, pre-existing app databases keep the old schema and then fail on the new `SELECT *`/upsert paths that assume `created_at` exists.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the scrutiny review/validator procedure so Task-spawned `scrutiny-feature-reviewer` subagents can run with automatic execution approval in this worker environment, or document the manual-review fallback explicitly.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: In this re-run, the Task invocation for `fix-skill-detail-install-timestamp` exited immediately with `Exec ended early: insufficient permission to proceed`, so the validator again had to perform the feature review manually after checking the handoff, commit diff, and schema behavior.
